metadata.title:
$AMEND SHERMAN TRUST LAW


TO HELP THE LABOR UNIONS

pages.raw_text:
|, fiscated by the court II damages
and costs are not paid promptly.
During the past year 80 inter-
national unions affiliated with the
American Federation of Labor re-
port having had total Of S61
strikes, in which 1923 persons
were involved, Of whom 1881
were benefited and 1892 secured
no benefits. The total cost of the
strikes Appropriated $2,550,0 900.
The trades In which the largest
number of persons were involved
were the miners, with 10000;
brewery workers, with 1850. and
hotel and restaurant employees,
1929, and telegraphers, 10000.


The trades that secured in
creases in wages with and without
strikes were the asbestos workers,
bill posters, broom makers, coop
ers, elevator constructors, station
airy firemen, foundry laborers, lad
les garment workers, granite cut
tiers, metal polishers, paving cut
tiers, printing pressmen, quarry
workers, street railway employees,
theatrical stage employees and
transfers. Reductions in hours of
labor were gained by BARRIERS, coop
ers, watch case engravers and
printing trades.


The 80 unions reporting made a
net gain of $500 in membership.
They also paid out $1,257,000 in
death benefits, s593,5OO in sick ben
, efits, $31 400 for deaths Of men,
bers' wives, $4,000 in travelling
benefits and $4,000 in tool insur-
ance.
